name: Vohu Manah
description: Vohu Manah, also known as Bahman in Middle Persian, is a central figure in Zoroastrianism, embodying the divine attribute of "Good Mind" or "Good Purpose." He is considered one of the Amesha Spentas, the six divine emanations or archangels of Ahura Mazda, the supreme god. Vohu Manah represents the divine wisdom and benevolent intention inherent in the cosmos. He is often depicted as the first creation of Ahura Mazda, reflecting his primordial role in the establishment of order and goodness. His presence guides individuals towards righteous thought, speech, and action, enabling them to discern truth from falsehood and to choose the path of virtue. Vohu Manah acts as an intermediary between Ahura Mazda and humanity, facilitating communication and understanding of the divine will. He is also associated with animals, particularly cattle, and is seen as their protector. Worshippers invoke Vohu Manah to cultivate inner peace, ethical discernment, and a commitment to moral living.
subtitle: Good Mind

keyMyths.title: The Encounter with Zarathushtra
keyMyths.description: According to Zoroastrian scriptures, Vohu Manah appeared to Zarathushtra (Zoroaster) at the age of thirty, leading him into the presence of Ahura Mazda. This pivotal encounter marked the beginning of Zarathushtra's prophetic mission. Vohu Manah guided Zarathushtra through the spiritual realms, revealing the nature of Ahura Mazda and the cosmic struggle between good and evil. This divine revelation empowered Zarathushtra to preach the teachings of Zoroastrianism and to challenge the prevailing religious practices of his time, ultimately establishing a new ethical and monotheistic faith.

keyMyths.title: The Battle Against Akem Manah
keyMyths.description: Vohu Manah is in constant opposition to Akem Manah, the "Evil Mind," representing negative thoughts, wicked intentions, and moral corruption. This cosmic battle between the forces of good and evil is a central theme in Zoroastrianism. Vohu Manah strives to counteract the influence of Akem Manah by inspiring individuals to cultivate positive thoughts, ethical conduct, and spiritual awareness. The choices individuals make in their daily lives contribute to the ongoing struggle between these opposing forces, ultimately determining the fate of the world.

keyMyths.title: Guidance of Souls After Death
keyMyths.description: After death, the soul is believed to undergo a spiritual judgment. Vohu Manah plays a role in guiding the souls of the righteous towards paradise, known as Garōdmān. He assists in assessing the merits of the deceased, ensuring that those who have lived a life of good thoughts, words, and deeds are rewarded with eternal bliss. This role underscores Vohu Manah's association with moral accountability and the ultimate triumph of good over evil in the afterlife.
